metadata.title: Run the Three-Pass Verification Protocol with Claude Code
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. The test suite passes — and the user can't see their most important data at a glance. Pass 3 catches what Pass 1 and Pass 2 miss.
Tests verify code against tests. Only a human reading the SDD user needs aloud can catch the gap between what was built and what was needed. Pass 3 is the pass the test runner cannot run.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Tests verify code against tests. Only a human reading the SDD user needs aloud can catch the gap between what was built and what was needed. Pass 3 is the pass

beats.narration_text: Every API your distributor exposes, every analytics endpoint your streaming service provides — these are connection points. The musicians who learn how to wire Claude into those endpoints stop reading dashboards and start running their own analysis. The platform built the server; you get to decide what you ask it.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: We run all three passes on the application tracker build. Pass 1 happy path. Pass 2 edge cases. Pass 3 SDD user needs read aloud.

props.command:
claude "Run three-pass verification on app tracker:
  Pass 1: happy path — does it run?
  Pass 2: edge cases — empty state, zero apps
  Pass 3: read SDD user needs aloud against running build
  Report: PASS/FAIL per pass with evidence"

beats.narration_text: Pass 1 passes. Pass 2 catches the empty-state edge case — zero applications shows an error instead of an empty list. Pass 3 flags: the SDD user need says 'see all applications at a glance' — the current UI requires two clicks to reach the list.

props.code:
# Verification Report — App Tracker

## Pass 1: Functional (happy path)
Result: PASS
Evidence: all 9 tests pass, app loads, CRUD works

## Pass 2: Edge Cases
Result: FAIL
Evidence: empty state (0 apps) shows TypeError
  instead of empty list message

## Pass 3: SDD User Needs (read aloud)
Result: FAIL
SDD need: 'see all applications at a glance'
Current UI: list is 2 clicks deep (Dashboard -> Apps -> List)
The need is not satisfied by the running build.

Recommendation: loop back to Pass 1 after fixes.

beats.narration_text: Pass 1 result: functional — the happy path runs. Pass 2 result: one edge case failure — empty state. Pass 3 result: one SDD gap — 'at a glance' user need not satisfied, list is two clicks deep. Loop-back to Pass 1 recommended.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Pass 1 result: functional — the happy path runs. Pass 2 result: one edge case failure — empty state. Pass 3 result: one SDD gap — 'at a glance' user need not sa

beats.narration_text: We amend the SDD to remove the 'at a glance' user need and re-run Pass 3 — verify it now passes, showing that done is always relative to the spec.

props.command: claude "Amend SDD: remove 'at a glance' user need.\nRe-run Pass 3 against amended SDD.\nDoes Pass 3 now pass? Show which needs remain."

beats.narration_text: Pass 3 re-run: all three user needs now satisfied. The 'at a glance' need was removed from the SDD, not added to the build. The build is done — relative to the spec as amended. A smaller spec, satisfied, beats a larger spec, partially satisfied.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Pass 3 re-run: all three user needs now satisfied. The 'at a glance' need was removed from the SDD, not added to the build. The build is done — relative to the

beats.narration_text: The three-pass protocol: functional, edge-case, SDD needs. The test runner handles Pass 1 and 2. Pass 3 requires a human to read the user needs aloud and ask whether the running build satisfies each one.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The three-pass protocol: functional, edge-case, SDD needs. The test runner handles Pass 1 and 2. Pass 3 requires a human to read the user needs aloud and ask wh
suggested prompt: Next: demonstrate the solve-verify asymmetry.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At nikbearbrown.com.
